title: Testing, Static Quality, Performance, and Release · Async Failure Triage
summary: Harden the service-data client with behavioral tests, deterministic fixtures, performance evidence, and a clean release. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a tested and release-ready JavaScript application and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Design tests around observable behavior, changed inputs, boundaries, and failures rather than implementation lines.
Use fakes, stubs, spies, and controlled clocks only at owned boundaries without mocking away the behavior under test.
Await asynchronous outcomes, control timers and network fixtures, and fail on unhandled work deterministically.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
// Add one runnable behavior, assertion, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Release Evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Release Evidence assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: A release candidate passes the familiar 3-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. Reproduce both, add behavior and accessibility regressions, and reject release until the clean build agrees. Competency probe: Produce a clean-environment build, supported-runtime record, accessibility checks, rollback plan, and stakeholder acceptance evidence.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Release Evidence has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Release Evidence precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A release candidate passes the familiar 3-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. Reproduce both, add behavior and accessibility regressions, and reject release until the clean build agrees. Competency probe: Produce a clean-environment build, supported-runtime record, accessibility checks, rollback plan, and stakeholder acceptance evidence.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A successful local run is sufficient release proof.

options.text: Declare inputs, observable output, ownership, and the runtime behavior being tested for the debug Release Evidence case
Trace values, bindings, receivers, mutations, and queued work before editing code for the debug Release Evidence case
Implement the smallest behavior and retain an assertion over the actual result for the debug Release Evidence case
Run boundary and changed-event cases, inspect failures, and remove accidental coupling for the debug Release Evidence case

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Add a evidence_js_release_evidence_l9iym3 function that derives the changed-case result, asserts one meaningful invariant with console.assert, and returns inspectable evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Release Evidence into observable performance evidence.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Release Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A release candidate passes the familiar 2-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. steps.why: Retrieving Release Evidence through the concrete a tested and release-ready JavaScript application case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Produce a clean-environment build, supported-runtime record, accessibility checks, rollback plan, and stakeholder acceptance evidence.
